steps.title: Rearrange and identify coefficients
steps.content: Start with $3x^2 - 6x = t$ and move $t$ to the left side to get the standard quadratic form $3x^2 - 6x - t = 0$. Here $a = 3$, $b = -6$, and $c = -t$.

steps.title: Apply the no-real-solutions condition
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solutions exactly when its discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ is negative. Substitute the coefficients:
$$(-6)^2 - 4(3)(-t) \lt 0$$
$$36 + 12t \lt 0$$
$$t \lt -3$$

steps.title: Test each choice against $t \lt -3$
steps.content:
• A) $t = -4$: is $-4 \lt -3$? Yes — no real solutions.
  • B) $t = -2$: is $-2 \lt -3$? No — eliminate.
  • C) $t = 2$: is $2 \lt -3$? No — eliminate.
  • D) $t = 4$: is $4 \lt -3$? No — eliminate.
That confirms A is correct.
